rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees involving ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope rules when inserting AGREE contracts: component types for port-based assumptions/guarantees, implementations for subcomponent bindings.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible features and ports in their respective scopes.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid in that scope.
Do not encode AADL timing properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: Defensive rules for Boolean data range comparison
Defensive rules for AADL property names
Defensive rules for event port numeric encoding
